description: FGAP-013 fix — CLI --json structured output (no double-encode). Introduce OpResult = string | {json:unknown} and widen OpDefinition.run. Split serializeForRead (read-element.ts) into structureForRead (data + cap/pagination metadata, un-stringified) + renderReadText (content + footer). 19 JSON.stringify ops return {json:result}; 16 serializeForRead ops return {json: structureForRead(...)}; 14 prose ops unchanged. Shared renderOpResultText used by registerAll (Pi-tool text byte-unchanged) and the CLI default branch; CLI --json emits typeof r===string ? r : r.json. Add a cli.test contract test locking --json output as a JSON value. Per the approved plan; one subsystem (op-output contract). Full completion sequence; coding subagent; orchestrator runs all npm + sole git writer.
acceptance_criteria: --json output is a JSON value (object/array) for a read op and a JSON.stringify op; still a string for a prose op
CLI default (non-json) output and in-pi Pi-tool text output byte-unchanged
a read op past the cap emits truncated/hasMore/total as structured --json fields, no [read-element] footer inside the JSON value
new cli.test contract test locks the --json value shape
full suite green; fresh adversarial audit confirms no op left double-encoding, no prose misclassified, text surfaces unchanged
FGAP-013 closed with the 4 closure fields
